data_sources.name: Missouri ER Visits for Tick Bites (2026 Spring Surge)
data_sources.description: Midwest (incl. Missouri) sharp rise: 39 per 100,000 in March → 137 per 100,000 in April 2026. Directly tied to Lone Star activity and rising AGS warnings. University Health (KC) opened dedicated AGS hotline.
data_sources.notes: Excellent real-time 2026 burden data for red layer.

data_sources.name: Oklahoma Alpha-gal Syndrome Mandatory Reporting (SB 1644, 2026)
data_sources.description: SB 1644 signed May 2026 — AGS becomes reportable Nov 1, 2026. Seroprevalence ~35%; estimated 70,000–140,000 potential full AGS cases. Multiple reports of repeated ER visits for anaphylaxis.
data_sources.notes: Strong legal + exposure data for South/Midwest AGS burden.

data_sources.name: Texas Tickborne Disease Cases (DSHS 2020–2026)
data_sources.description: Low Lyme (21 cases in 2024, 19 in 2025 YTD); Ehrlichiosis steady (13–14 cases/year). Lone Star tick widespread — expected high AGS burden despite low reported Lyme.
data_sources.notes: Hard numbers showing Texas as low Lyme but high expected AGS/Ehrlichia region.

notes: Most other items (CDC Tick Bite Tracker, Missouri AGS estimate, MMWR, military seroprevalence) were already integrated from prior dumps. This batch adds fresh 2026 ER surge data, OK mandatory reporting law + case estimates, and Texas DSHS infection counts. Excellent for red layer infection/ER burden and reinforcing 'South = AGS/Ehrlichia heavy' narrative.
